front: Empire: named for the North Indian city of Delhi, its capital; ruled by dynasties such as the Tughlaq and Lodi
Empire: succeeded by the Mughal Empire, which followed its final Lodi dynasty
Empire: ruled from what is now the capital of India
Empire: ruling dynasties included the Burji and Khilji
Empire: descended from conquests made by Muhammad of Ghor, and was an enemy of the Rajputs; collective name for the Mamluk, Khalji, Tughlaq, Sayyid, and Lodi dynasties
Event: established as a result of the Battles of Taraori and Chandawar and other campaigns of Qutb al-Din Aibak
Empire: briefly re-established by Sher Shah Suri after Ibrahim Lodi fell to Babur at Panipat
Figure: founded the Delhi Sultanate in 1206 as a former slave general of the Ghurid dynasty; began the Qutb Minar and first ruled from Lahore
work: Founding: Ghurid Conquest & the Mamluk (Slave) Dynasty

front: Figure: stabilized the early Delhi Sultanate and named his daughter Razia as his heir
work: Founding: Ghurid Conquest & the Mamluk (Slave) Dynasty

front: Ruler: seized power by donning a red garment (kaghzi pirhan) symbolizing seekers of justice and inciting the public against her stepmother Shah Turkan
work: Founding: Ghurid Conquest & the Mamluk (Slave) Dynasty

front: Ruler: referenced the Queen of Sheba on coins inscribed "Bilqis-i Jihan"; a contemporary lamented "black dust" on her clothing
work: Founding: Ghurid Conquest & the Mamluk (Slave) Dynasty

front: Ruler: overthrown amid a rumored romance with the Abyssinian slave Jamal ud-Din Yaqut
work: Founding: Ghurid Conquest & the Mamluk (Slave) Dynasty

front: Institution: council of Turkic slave-nobles that administered the early Delhi Sultanate until it was destroyed by the sultan Balban
work: Founding: Ghurid Conquest & the Mamluk (Slave) Dynasty

front: Event: Bughra Khan refused this state's leadership to keep his own governorship; his inept son Qaiqabad became ruler instead and fell to the Khalji Revolution
Figure: an enslaved general who briefly seized the Delhi Sultanate's throne, attempted to convert it to his own religion, and was swiftly overthrown; the historian Barani was greatly critical of him
Ruler: fixed prices and banned hoarding through the Shahana-i-Mandi and Diwan-i-Riyasat systems
Ruler: raised the khums tax on war booty to 80% and the kharaj tax on peasant produce to 50%, using the revenue to build the water tank Hauz Khas
Event: general Zafar Khan died at this battle, which repulsed the last of a series of Chagatai Khanate invasions
Ruler: this Delhi Sultanate ruler repeatedly repelled Mongol invasions
Ruler: the Delhi Sultanate reached its greatest territorial extent under this ruler
Event: the Delhi Sultanate briefly moved its capital to this city in the Deccan
Ruler: employed the traveler Ibn Battuta as a judge (qadi) for six years

Event: historian Ziauddin Barani describes how the Delhi Sultanate's rulers built four of the so-called "seven older cities" of the capital, including the Siri Fort complex and the district of Firozabad
Event: the Delhi Sultanate's capital was sacked by this conqueror in 1398
Figure: rose to power by falsely claiming descent from Muhammad, establishing the short-lived Sayyid Dynasty, which was later overthrown by the Lodi family
Ruler: this first Lodi ruler created a succession crisis by naming his son Babrak as viceroy
Ruler: wrote poetry under the pseudonym Gulrukhi and founded the city of Agra as an alternate capital
Ruler: destroyed an idol at the Nagarkot temple and gave its pieces to butchers to use as weights; took Gwalior Fort from the Tomar dynasty
Ruler: the final ruler of the Delhi Sultanate, defeated and killed at the First Battle of Panipat by Babur
Event: the Delhi Sultanate's last dynasty, the Lodis, were conquered by Babur and the Mughals at this 1526 battle
Document: written by Ziya ud-Din Barani, advocated sorting citizens into the ashraf and ajlaf classes
Figure: this poet was employed by one ruler of the Delhi Sultanate
Event: forces under Alauddin Khalji looted the Hoysala capital of this city and besieged the Pandya stronghold of Madurai
Event: Delhi Sultanate invasions helped collapse the Hoysala Empire, whose remnants gave rise to this southern successor empire
Event: Delhi Sultanate raids weakened the Reddi kingdom and exacerbated the 14th-century decline of this Tamil dynasty
